execution_summary: I created replication_info.json and a Python entrypoint (main.py) that replicates the Rmd analysis, generated the Dockerfile, built and ran the container, and executed the analysis. The script reads JSON subject files, constructs trial-level variables (dangerous_bee, correct, 1D correctness), identifies test-phase trials (with a fallback), aggregates per-subject 1D accuracy, and runs an independent-samples t-test comparing contingent vs full-information conditions. No major deviations from the preregistration logic were introduced, aside from robust handling of JSON structure and fallbacks when fields were missing.

code_executed.logs: N contingent: 6 N full: 6\nt= 0.5917723823643538 p= 0.5671399978982098\nWrote replication_output_test_summary.csv
code_executed.environment: Python 3.9-slim container with pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels installed (via replication_info.json pip spec).

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants in the contingent feedback condition will adopt an inferior one-dimension categorization strategy more often than participants in the full-information condition (higher average 1D score in contingent).

tables.table_description: Per-subject acc_2d, acc_1d_a, acc_1d_b, and acc_1d aggregated by condition were written.
